title: MANGA Neon Genesis Evangelion Gakuen Datenroku
display_title: MANGA Neon Genesis Evangelion Gakuen Datenroku
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] Neon Genesis Evangelion Gakuen Datenroku             Quote Post by klasnic » Fri Sep 07, 2007 10:09 am A new manga of Evangelion is going to sell in the Asuka magazine,the same that published GOS2.The autor of this manga is Min Nemuri.The first stage was published the 24 August. Some images of this manga: http://xs319.xs.to/xs319/07365/213.jpg http://xs219.xs.to/xs219/07365/254.jpg http://xs219.xs.to/xs219/07365/464.jpg http://xs219.xs.to/xs219/07365/4646.jpg http://xs219.xs.to/xs219/07365/6464.jpg http://xs219.xs.to/xs219/07365/64654.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Sep 07, 2007 10:32 am They're moving into the action market now, huh? Well, the art looks okay.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Sep 07, 2007 10:36 am Just when you think that there hasn't been any new Eva-ish lately, here it comes! :P Yes, the art is quite good.

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Fri Sep 07, 2007 11:12 am It looks...interesting. So the Angels were first re-imagined as scantily-clad teenage girls, and now they're leather-wearing bishounen? Oh well, at least it's some eye candy for the ladies.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Fri Sep 07, 2007 11:43 am Looks very cool, definately something that will be taking my money when ADV Manga (or whoever) decide to release it over here! There's a pretty suggestive picture of Kaworu and Shinji in the first image there - so I'm guessing this isn't exactly going to fit into the shoenen category. Also, all the characters look a little older, no?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 07, 2007 11:45 am this doesnt look like eva

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 07, 2007 12:22 pm I was wondering where those pictures were coming from as I have been seeing those images around for a week or so. Just another glorified dojin to get people to spend money on.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Sep 07, 2007 12:57 pm DatDude wrote: This doesnt look like eva.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Sep 07, 2007 2:09 pm This = Evangelion + Tsukihime? Gakuen Mokushiroku = Highschool of the Dead? New Eva Manga for me to collect. Yay.[/sarcasm]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 07, 2007 3:26 pm I said it on EML and I'll say it here: This seriously makes me think of Eva meets the Matrix, though I can't recall/haven't watched all of the latter... ;

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Fri Sep 07, 2007 3:30 pm CitizenGeek wrote: There's a pretty suggestive picture of Kaworu and Shinji in the first image there - so I'm guessing this isn't exactly going to fit into the shoenen category.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 07, 2007 3:52 pm I think so. It IS what the GoS2 Manga ran in, and that had a Shoujo-flavor IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Sep 07, 2007 4:46 pm I'm gonna regret saying this, but... I actually like the look of this. What can I say? I've waited ages to see Shinji actually hold a gun. What's it called?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Sep 07, 2007 4:49 pm Btw, am i the only one that thinks that Kaworu on the cover looks exactly like Near from Death Note?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 07, 2007 4:50 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: What's it called?

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Sep 07, 2007 4:52 pm As in translated. I don't know much in the way of Japanese. And yes, he does look a bit like that. Do you think there are any correlations there?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Fri Sep 07, 2007 6:11 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Btw, am i the only one that thinks that Kaworu on the cover looks exactly like Near from Death Note?

[]:             Quote Post by marmotgirl » Fri Sep 07, 2007 7:07 pm CitizenGeek wrote: So, Asuka only does shoujo series, then?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Sep 07, 2007 7:13 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: As in translated. I don't know much in the way of Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Shawn Dudley » Fri Sep 07, 2007 7:16 pm Super Bishonen Eva - my shonen-ai otaku daughter's dream come true!
